properties.media:imagetext:
REV.11/2001
AmericanAirlines 767-300
IMPORTANT SAFETY INFORMATION
ATTENTION: IF YOU ARE SEATED IN AN EXIT ROW AND CANNOT UNDERSTAND CREW COMMANDS OR THE INFORMATION ON THIS CARD,
PLEASE CONTACT A FLIGHT ATTENDANT.
ATTENTION! SI VOUS ÊTES ASSIS PRÈS D'UNE ISSUE DE SECOURS ET NE COMPRENEZ PAS LES INSTRUCTIONS DE
L'ÉQUIPAGE OU LES INFORMATIONS QUI SE TROUVENT SUR CE FORMULAIRE, VOUS ÉTES PRIÉS DE BIEN VOULOIR VOUS
METTRE EN CONTACT AVEC LE PERSONNEL DU BORD.
ACHTUNG: FALLS SICH IHR SITZ IN EINER REIHE AN EINEM DER AUSGÄNGE BEFINDET UND SIE SCHWIERIGKEITEN HABEN, DIE
ANWEISUNGEN DER BESATZUNG ODER DIE INFORMATIONEN AUF DIESER KARTE ZU VERSTEHEN, SETZEN SIE SICH BITTE MIT EINEM
FLUGBEGLEITER IN VERBINDUNG.
ATENCION, SI USTED ESTÁ SENTADO EN UNA FILA DE SALIDA Y NO ENTIENDE LAS INDICACIONES DE LA TRIPULACIÓN O LA
INFORMACIÓN EN ESTA TARJETA, POR FAVOR PONGASE EN CONTACTO CON ALGUIEN DE LA TRIPULACIÓN DE SERVICIO
In the event of an emergency, if you are seated in an exit row you may be called upon to open the exit and
assist fellow passengers in exiting the aircraft if a crew member is not available to do so. Please review the
following information carefully.
Exit Row Criteria:
For the benefit of all passengers, Federal
Law requires that passengers seated in exit
rows meet the following criteria:
IF YOU ARE SEATED IN AN EXIT ROW
PLEASE ASK TO BE RESEATED IF:
1. You lack sufficient mobility, strength, or
dexterity in both arms and hands, and
both legs...
• To reach upward,
sideways, and
downward to the location of
emergency exit and exit slide
operating mechanisms.
To grasp and push, pull, turn or
otherwise operate those mechanisms.
• To push, shove, pull, or otherwise
open emergency exits.
• To lift out, hold, and store in the
proper location objects the size and
weight of over-wing window exit doors.
• To remove obstructions similar in size
and weight to over-wing exit doors.
• To reach the emergency exit quickly.
• To maintain balance while removing
obstructions.
• To exit quickly.
• To stabilize escape slide after
deployment, (on aircraft equipped with
escape slides,) or
• To assist others in getting off an
escape slide (on aircraft equipped
with escape slides.)
2.
You are less than 15 years of age or lack
Exit Row Responsibilities:
the ability to perform one or more of these
functions without the assistance of an
• Follow oral directions and hand signals
adult companion, parent, or other relative.
given by crew members.
3.
You lack the ability to read and understand
• Locate the emergency exit.
instructions related to emergency
• ASSESS OUTSIDE CONDITIONS -
evacuations provided on this card in
IF OPENING EXIT INCREASES THE
printed or graphic form or you lack the
HAZARD TO PASSENGERS, DO NOT
ability to understand oral crew member
OPEN.
commands.
• Recognize the emergency exit opening
You lack sufficient visual capacity to
handle.
perform one or more of the applicable
• Follow the instructions provided on the exit
functions without the assistance of visual
for proper operation.
aids other than contact lenses or eye
glasses.
• Operate the emergency exit.
•Stow or secure the emergency exit door
5. You lack sufficient hearing capacity to hear
and understand instructions shouted by
so that it will not impede use of the exit.
crew members without assistance other
•On craft equipped with escape slides,
than a hearing aid.
assess the condition of the escape slide.
6. You lack the ability to adequately
Inflate the slide and stabilize the slide after
deployment to assist others in getting off
communicate intormation orally to other
the slide.
passengers, or
7. You have a condition or responsibilities,
Pass quickly through the emergency exit.
such as caring for small children, that
• Assess,
select, and follow a safe path
might prevent you from performing one or
away from the emergency exit.
more of these functions, or
You have a condition that might cause you
harm if you perform one or more of these
functions.
9. You have a condition that is
unrecognizable to others that will prevent
you from performing the functions listed
above or which might result in harm to you
if you perform one of these functions, or
10. You do not wish to perform these
functions.
EXIT OPERATION: ASSESS OUTSIDE CONDITIONS-IF OPENING EXIT INCREASES
THE HAZARD TO PASSENGERS, DO NOT OPEN.
Please Do Not Remove This Card From Airplane
